statusPolicy.ready: Static acceptance contract and supporting checks are present now.
statusPolicy.pending: Visible in the machine-readable suite, but not yet release-blocking in visibility mode.
statusPolicy.blocked: Visible with an explicit blocker and next action; publish mode fails until resolved.
statusPolicy.publishModeRule: In --gate=publish, every blocking test must be ready.

requiredTerms: SmartArt creation and full SmartArt editing are unsupported
unsupported; overlays do not physically remove underlying content
Overlay text or rectangles are not redaction.
Underlying PDF content is not removed
Complete DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, and PDF application-level editing engines

tests.title: Agent capability truthfulness contract

steps.expect: Static gate confirms agent-visible capabilities expose limitation-bearing contract fields.
Core capabilities, CLI metadata, and wired command exposure remain aligned.

passCriteria: Agent-visible capability metadata cannot claim broad complete Office/PDF support without limitations.
Capability truthfulness checks are machine-readable and tied to remediation IDs.

tests.title: Unsupported EditOps remain rejected

steps.expect: Schema tests reject broad chart, pivot, slicer, and PDF redaction payloads.
Runtime schemas and implemented EditOps remain represented in the schema registry.

passCriteria: Unsupported operations are absent from accepted EditOps.
Rejected payloads fail structurally rather than becoming silent no-ops.

tests.title: PDF overlay is not redaction

steps.expect: Capabilities and README keep physical redaction/content rewrite unsupported.
Schema tests reject redaction-like PDF payloads.

passCriteria: No acceptance evidence may mark an overlay as physical redaction.
Capability disclosures and schema behavior agree.

tests.title: Release gate and traceability floor

steps.expect: Perfect spec suite covers the v3 remediation matrix and exposes pending or blocked work.

steps.run: npm run version:check && npm run pack:smoke
steps.expect: Version surfaces and packaged CLI smoke checks pass before tagging.

passCriteria: Every v3 remediation row is represented by at least one L7 acceptance item.
Release gate commands remain visible in machine-readable evidence.

tests.title: End-to-end evidence bundle manifest

steps.expect: Acceptance workflow writes a manifest, summary, event log, and static check output.

passCriteria: Manifest lists every L7 acceptance item with ready, pending, or blocked state.
Generated artifacts include bytes and sha256 where applicable.

tests.title: README feature claim parity

steps.expect: README limitation wording remains aligned with capability truthfulness checks.

passCriteria: README copy uses scoped language for charts, SmartArt, pivot/slicer, PDF, and DOCX support.
README does not outrun agent-visible capabilities.

tests.title: Office workflow limitation evidence

steps.run: npm run perfect-spec:evidence && npm run perfect-spec:check
steps.expect: Fixture-level evidence manifest records current implemented PPTX, DOCX, XLSX, and PDF coverage with source hashes.

passCriteria: Unsupported constructs are disclosed as limitations rather than silently passing.
PPTX, DOCX, XLSX, and PDF each have at least one fixture evidence entry with test name, command, target feature, source sha256/bytes, and status.
Asset replacement behavior remains covered by existing command behavior or explicit not-applicable evidence.

tests.title: Native renderer optionality remains explicit

steps.run: npm run perfect-spec:evidence && npm run perfect-spec:check
steps.expect: Native renderer evidence records doctor availability, default external-process denial, and optional native gate requirements without requiring native conversion success.

passCriteria: Native renderer absence does not block non-native release gates.
Optional native evidence is never reported as universally available.
Default policy evidence confirms native conversion is blocked while externalProcess is deny.
Tag/release runners that claim native fidelity must document Office COM or LibreOffice availability and opt in to trusted native renderer policy.

tests.title: Post-tag install smoke is classified
tests.statusReason: GitHub tag and remote install smoke can only run after a tag or remote target exists.
tests.nextAction: Run github-install tag/remote smoke after publishing and attach logs to release evidence.

steps.run: npm run github-install:tag-smoke && npm run github-install:remote-smoke
steps.expect: Post-tag install paths succeed against the published release.

passCriteria: Pre-tag gate does not pretend post-tag checks have already passed.
Post-tag smoke remains visible as release follow-up evidence.

tests.title: Remediation matrix coverage

steps.expect: Every remediation matrix row is covered by at least one L7 test or an explicit coverage exclusion.

passCriteria: Coverage is computed from remediation IDs, not prose.
Unknown remediation IDs in the suite fail the static gate.

tests.title: Human and agent output limitation parity

steps.run: npm run perfect-spec:cli-parity && npm run perfect-spec:check
steps.expect: CLI parity capture records version, capabilities, schema list, render, inspect, and view transcripts with stdout hashes and parsed status.

passCriteria: Human output does not claim success beyond agent JSON status.
Agent JSON does not drop limitation-bearing capability fields.
Each captured output records command, exitCode, stdout bytes, stdout sha256, parsed schema id, and parsed ok status.

tests.title: Publish mode non-ready escalation

steps.expect: Future publish gate fails while blocking L7 items remain pending or blocked.

passCriteria: Visibility mode can report non-ready work without failing.
Publish mode has a deterministic path to fail unresolved blocking acceptance items.
